era_context: Middle Bronze Age. The Hyksos continue their rule in Egypt; Mycenaean Greece is in the shaft-grave period; the Hittite Old Kingdom is consolidating Anatolian power; Babylon is under Kassite or late Amorite rule; the Shang dynasty is established in China. This era is characterized by palace-centered economies, the spread of chariot warfare, and expanding long-distance trade.
disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified; no events are attested for this year.
historiographic_note: The year 1618 BCE has no known contemporary documentation. The Middle Bronze Age chronology remains uncertain, and most years in this period are archaeologically invisible unless they coincide with major events (destructions, dynastic transitions, or natural disasters) that are later recorded or archaeologically dated.
